title: Chocolate mirror glaze
subtitle: Gelatine-set cocoa glaze poured over a frozen cake for a reflective finish
excerpt: A chocolate mirror glaze is made from cocoa, cream, sugar, and gelatine. It is poured at 32–35°C over a frozen cake and sets immediately on contact, producing a perfectly smooth, reflective surface. The cake must be frozen solid before glazing; a warm cake causes the glaze to run and pool.
sourceNotes: Gelée au miroir (mirror glaze) is a French patisserie technique documented in professional confectionery manuals from the mid-twentieth century. The domestic adaptation using gelatine leaves, cocoa powder, cream, and sugar is now widely reproduced in English-language baking references and is fully public-domain in its basic form. The key technical parameters are glaze temperature at pour (32 to 35°C) and the need for a frozen surface; these are derived from standard confectionery science and appear in all substantive treatments of the technique.

recipe.yieldDescription: enough to glaze a 20 cm entremet or round cake
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The glaze can be made ahead and refrigerated for up to 5 days. Reheat gently to melt, cool to 32–35°C, and use immediately.

baking.decoratingTechnique: mirror glaze — poured at 32–35°C over frozen cake

glossaryTerms.definition: The glaze must be poured at exactly 32–35°C onto a frozen cake surface. At this temperature the gelatine is still fluid but cool enough to set on contact with the frozen cake. Above 35°C and it stays liquid too long, running off before it can set. Below 30°C and it thickens before reaching the base of the cake, creating a lumpy surface. Check with an instant-read thermometer. If the glaze is too warm, set the bowl in cold water and stir; if too cool, microwave in 5-second bursts. Pour in one continuous motion from a height of about 20 cm for the best coverage.

content.text: Mirror glaze is a precision technique. The glaze temperature and the cake temperature at the moment of pouring determine everything. Prepare the glaze ahead; freeze the cake for at least 4 hours; check the thermometer just before you pour.

items.prepNote: soaked in cold water for 5 minutes

content.text: Combine the {{granulated-sugar}}, {{whole-milk}}, and {{double-cream}} in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then sift in the {{cocoa-powder}} and whisk until smooth. Return to a simmer. Remove from the heat and add the squeezed-out {{gelatine-leaves}}, stirring until dissolved. Pass through a fine sieve into a tall jug. Blend briefly with the stick blender (held below the surface to avoid air bubbles), then leave to cool to 32–35°C.
Set the cake on a rack, pour glaze
Remove the frozen cake from the freezer and place it on a wire cooling rack set over a tray (to catch drips). Check the glaze temperature using the

guidance: it must read 32–35°C. Pour the glaze in one steady stream from a height of about 20 cm, moving slowly across the top and letting it fall naturally down the sides. Do not touch the surface once poured. Transfer to the fridge for 20 minutes until the glaze is set but still soft.
